title: trifolium ochroleucon subsp. caucasicum
intro: Trifolium ochroleucon subsp. caucasicum, also known as the Caucasian white clover, is a subspecies of white clover that belongs to the legume family and is native to the Caucasus region. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as combat infections. The health benefits of Trifolium ochroleucon subsp. caucasicum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
